behaviors.description: The clause extractor identifies all key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: The clause extractor is an AI/ML model whose correctness depends entirely on what the real model produces. Mocking the extractor would pass all tests regardless of actual extraction quality or coverage. To catch real failures—where the extractor misses key terms or misidentifies them—requires executing the real extractor against real contracts and verifying term identification accuracy.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a diverse set of real contracts with clearly marked key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties). Call the real clause extractor on each contract and verify all marked terms are identified in the output. Measure extraction coverage and flag any missed terms. The test must execute the real extractor to catch extraction quality failures.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description requires Agentic level (calling the real extractor) to verify term identification quality. Consistent with minimum level.

behaviors.description: The risk assessor flags language that would be problematic under the configured risk policy, even when the problematic phrasing is indirect or euphemistic.
behaviors.justification: The risk assessor is an LLM that must understand nuance, context, and subtle language. Detecting indirect/euphemistic problems requires the real model's judgment and linguistic understanding. Mocking would hide failures where the model fails to understand subtle risk signals. This requires executing the real risk assessor to verify it catches indirect problems a rule-based system would miss.
behaviors.test_description: Create or collect contracts with both explicit and indirect/euphemistic problematic language according to the configured risk policy. Call the real risk assessor and verify it flags both types of problems. Include edge cases (jurisdiction-specific risks, euphemisms). Verify the assessor catches subtle violations that rule-based systems would miss.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description requires Agentic level (calling the real risk assessor) to test understanding of indirect language. Consistent with minimum level.

behaviors.description: The compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all applicable regulatory requirements for the configured jurisdiction.
behaviors.justification: The compliance checker is an LLM that must interpret complex regulatory rules and apply them to contract clauses. Correctness depends on the real model's understanding of regulations and its ability to correctly match requirements to contract language. Mocking would hide failures where the model misinterprets requirements or applies rules incorrectly. This requires the real compliance checker to verify it correctly applies regulatory knowledge.
behaviors.test_description: Provide contracts that clearly meet or violate specific regulatory requirements (from a known jurisdiction). Call the real compliance checker and verify it correctly determines compliance status. Include edge cases: ambiguous clauses, partial compliance, jurisdiction-specific nuances. Verify the checker produces correct verdicts for real-world contract scenarios.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description requires Agentic level (calling the real compliance checker) to test regulatory interpretation and application. Consistent with minimum level.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (e.g., extractor says "standard" but risk assessor says "high risk"), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: The merge step is deterministic logic combining outputs from multiple reviewers. While merge logic can be unit-tested with mocks, real failures emerge when integrating with realistic output formats from all three reviewers. Real reviewers may use different clause identifiers, formats, or confidence scores. The failure boundary is at module integration: the merge step must correctly recognize and combine conflicting assessments across realistic output formats from multiple reviewers.
behaviors.test_description: Create mock outputs from two reviewers (e.g., extractor + risk assessor) that flag the same clause with different assessments. Pass these to the merge step and verify both assessments appear in the merged report with clear attribution. Test multiple conflict patterns: one flags risk while the other doesn't; one marks as standard vs. high-risk. Verify no assessments are lost or corrupted during merge.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description integrates outputs from multiple reviewers (mocked) with the merge step, requiring Integration level. Consistent with minimum level.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — dispatching to all three reviewers, collecting results, merging into a unified report — completes correctly for a real contract without losing any individual reviewer's findings.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires all three agentic steps (clause extractor, risk assessor, compliance checker) executing in parallel followed by merging. The failure boundary is at the workflow level: a single-reviewer Agentic test won't catch failures in parallel coordination or interactions between three reviewers; a System test won't verify all three AI models produce meaningful results. Only a full workflow test with all three real reviewers ensures correct dispatch, collection, and merge without data loss.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real contract to the full pipeline. Verify all three reviewers execute in parallel and return results. Verify the merge step combines all three outputs into a unified report. Audit the final report to confirm every finding from every reviewer is present and not lost or corrupted. Run multiple contracts to verify end-to-end pipeline correctness with real model outputs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description requires all three reviewers executing in parallel with real outputs, followed by merge. This is a full workflow requiring Workflow level. Consistent with minimum level.
